hud.streakAtRiskTitle: Clear a step today so your streak survives.

streakPopover.count: {days, plural, =0 {No streak yet} =1 {1 day in a row} other {# days in a row}}

rules.justOpen: Just opening the app doesn't count

streakPopover.freezes: {count, plural, =1 {You have 1 streak shield in reserve} other {You have # streak shields in reserve}}
streakPopover.atRiskNudge: Your streak is at risk today. The 1-minute daily mission locks it in.
streakPopover.footer: Skip a day and it resets to 0

streakSaveBanner.message: {count, plural, =1 {Your streak shield covered 1 day. The streak survives.} other {Your streak shields covered # days. The streak survives.}}

xpPopover.count: {xp, plural, =0 {No XP yet} =1 {1 XP earned} other {# XP earned}}

rules.firstTry: +12 XP for clearing a step on the first try
rules.laterTry: +8 XP for clearing on a later try
rules.capstones: Capstones grant a bigger XP push

xpPopover.footer: More XP, closer to next promotion

gemsPopover.count: {gems, plural, =0 {No gems yet} =1 {1 gem in wallet} other {# gems in wallet}}

rules.earnFirstTry: +3 gems each time you clear a step on the first try
rules.earnCapstones: Bigger gem rewards from courses and capstones
rules.spendShop: Spend on hearts refill, streak freeze, or ask the crew

rules.hintsCost: Asking for a hint costs 1 heart
rules.passive: Reading lessons or running drills doesn't cost a heart

heartsPopover.footer: In a hurry? Refill in the Shop

profile.heading: Your profile is waiting
profile.body: Sign in to see your progress, XP, streak, and the achievements you'll unlock with the crew.

practice.heading: Practice, tailored to you
practice.body: The practice queue is built from the steps you're learning. Sign in so the crew can put your session together.

daily.heading: Daily mission
daily.body: Daily missions are assigned based on your progress. Sign in to receive yours and keep the streak alive.

shop.heading: Sign in to redeem
shop.body: Buying gem packs or using boosters needs an account first. It's free and takes 30 seconds.

workbench.title: Your prompt bench.
workbench.body: Free space to test prompts. No XP, no rubric, no penalty for retrying. just you, the model, and the loop. Forge keeps your last six runs so you can compare versions.

practice.title: Practice replays.
practice.body: Echo is calibrating a review mode: past drills with fresh variations to keep your reflexes sharp. Not open yet. check back when Echo gives the green light.

crew.title: Meet your crew.
crew.body: Soon you'll be able to read each member's full dossier. Vega, Atlas, Echo, Forge, Orbit, Hex. backstory, specialty, recurring lines. For now you meet them inside the lessons.

leaderboard.title: Fleet leaderboard.
leaderboard.body: Atlas is crunching weekly rankings. who keeps their streak, who clears the most capstones, who hangs onto their hearts. First publish drops once there's enough fleet.

shop.title: Base shop.
shop.body: Vega is haggling with the quartermaster: insignia cosmetics, heart refills, tokens for extra retries. Hold onto your gems. opens soon.

notFound.title: This deck doesn't exist.
notFound.body: Echo scanned the manifest. Nothing here matches the address you came from. Head back to the bridge and try a different route.

error.title: Something cracked on the bridge.
error.body: Forge is on it. Try again. most of the time it's a passing glitch. If it persists, ping the crew.

shop.title: Power-ups & boosters
shop.subtitle: Insurance and refills only. never a skip on evaluation.
shop.greeting: Welcome aboard. Stock up on the gear you need to keep the streak alive.

recommend.heart-refill: Your hearts are running low. Grab a refill so you can keep going without waiting for the regen.
recommend.heart-pack: Hearts are stable. top them all off before your next long session so nothing slows you down.
recommend.streak-freeze: No streak shields in stock. Stack one before a busy day so a skipped session doesn't reset your run.

streak-freeze.name: Streak shield
streak-freeze.description: If you skip a day, this shield absorbs the hit and your streak survives. Consumed automatically when needed. Stackable. buy several if you're traveling.

heart-refill.name: Heart refill (×1)
heart-refill.description: Restore one heart right now. Useful when you're one short of finishing an exercise and don't want to wait for the regen.

heart-pack.name: Heart pack (full)
heart-pack.description: Refill all hearts to max at once. Useful when starting a long session.

feedback.alreadyFull: Hearts are already at max. no need to buy.
feedback.unauthorized: You need to sign in to buy.
feedback.invalidInput: Something went wrong with the purchase. Try again.

gems.subtitle: One-time payment. Gems never expire and you can spend them on hearts, shields, and future power-ups.

feedback.comingSoon: Payments rolling out soon. We're wiring up checkout.
feedback.stripeError: Couldn't open checkout. Please try again.
feedback.unauthorized: You need to sign in to buy.

status.successBody: Gems will land in your wallet in a few seconds. If more than a minute passes, refresh the page.
status.cancelBody: You left checkout before paying. Try again whenever you want.

shop.philosophy: The philosophy: no item lets you skip evaluation. Hints, exercises, and rubrics are always free.

hero.eyebrow: Learn applied AI, not theoretical AI
hero.heading: Read on the left. Practice on the right. AI grades what your prompt actually achieves.
hero.sub: Naveo is the gamified platform for getting good at prompt engineering, MCPs, agents and complex flows. Real practice, instant feedback.
hero.note: First step is free. No card. No install.

preview.breadcrumb: Prompt anatomy · L1 · Role and context

reading.title: Role and context
reading.body: A good prompt starts by telling the model who it is and what problem it solves. Without that, it hallucinates.
reading.snippetRole: You are a strict data extractor

exercise.question: Which of the two prompts will force clean JSON?
exercise.optionB: Return ONLY JSON. No text before or after. Schema: '{' name, age, email '}'
exercise.feedback: B locks the format. The rubric verifies the model doesn't preamble.

loop.title: Three moves. No video, no slides, no waiting.

read.title: Read on the left
read.body: A short page with the principle, the pattern and an anti-pattern. No fifteen minutes of theory before you touch anything.

practice.title: Practice on the right
practice.body: You write the prompt, pick the option, label the anatomy or design the tool schema. The exercise changes with the skill.

judge.title: The rubric grades you
judge.body: We don't compare your prompt to a fixed answer. We grade what your prompt achieves against a rubric with an LLM judge.

rubric.title: We don't grade your prompt. We grade what your prompt achieves.
rubric.sub: Ninety percent of what we teach has no single right answer. That's why the heart of the product is a rubric with an LLM judge, not a string comparator.
rubric.promptBody:
You are a data extractor.
From the text below, return JSON with name, age, email.
If a field is missing, leave it as null.

Text: "Carlos, no email on file"
rubric.promptNote: Case 3 fails: the model invents a plausible email.

hallucinate.label: Does not hallucinate missing data
hallucinate.hint: When the email is missing, send null instead of inventing one.

shape.label: One line of JSON, no preamble

tracks.title: Four tracks. One progression.
tracks.sub: You start with the foundational skill and stack up to orchestrating agents.

prompting.title: Prompt anatomy
prompting.body: Role, context, examples, structured format. The skill everything else stacks on top of.

conversation.title: Conversations that work
conversation.body: Memory, follow-ups, recovering bad outputs. Driving an LLM multi-turn toward a concrete goal.

mcps.title: Tools and MCPs
mcps.body: You design tools the LLM actually decides to use. Your description is what the model reads. Unique content nobody else teaches.

flows.title: Complex flows
flows.body: Chains, routing, agents, evals. When a single prompt isn't enough.

stations.title: Each crew member runs a station. Their authority comes from knowing.
stations.sub: Not decoration. Your progress is measured per station: you master Comms with Vega, Engineering with Forge, Security with Hex. Each track unlocks more coordination.

atlas.name: Atlas signs your capstones
atlas.body: Atlas has no station. He crosses all of them. He shows up at the end of each track and, if you pass, he logs it. If not, he sends you to practice with the right person.

vega.name: Vega
vega.body: The voice that teaches you to structure prompts. Role, context, examples, format. You start hearing her at step one.

echo.name: Echo
echo.body: Conversation memory, surgical corrections, recovering outputs without losing what worked. She doesn't rewrite, she edits.

forge.name: Forge
forge.body: The workshop. Tool schemas, descriptions the LLM understands, MCPs, error handling. Where ideas become tools.

orbit.name: Orbit
orbit.body: Chains, routing, agents with a loop. Orchestrates multi-step missions and decides when to parallelize.

hex.name: Hex
hex.body: Direct and indirect injection, leaks, adversarial validation. If your system touches users, Hex audits it.

daily.title: A short quest every day. From things you've already seen.
daily.sub: Five to eight scenes, two or three minutes, one concept that reinforces your latest lesson. The streak lives here.

bullets.length: Five to eight mini exercises, not a 30-second quiz.
bullets.bias: Biased to concepts you've already seen. You never get topics you haven't learned yet.
bullets.streak: Daily streak, lives and gems. The ritual compounds.

card.label: Today with Echo
card.title: Surgical correction on a prompt
card.body: The user asked for a change. You edit without breaking the rest of the output.

progress.title: Everything you do is measured. And visible.
progress.sub: XP, streak, lives, gems and mastery per station. Plus the weekly league against everyone else on board.

cta.title: Your first shift starts with Vega.
cta.sub: First step is free and takes five minutes. If you like how it feels, you keep going. If not, you lost nothing.

list.subheading: Each track is a series of steps with the crew member who owns that protocol. Mix freely. Echo's checklist is unlocked from your first hello.

card.phrasePreview: What you'll be able to do by the end of this track

globalPath.title: Your path through the fleet
globalPath.subtitle: Each track is a world. You clear them left-to-right and down. at the end, Atlas is waiting on the bridge.

cta.bossNote: Unlocks once every track is cleared

debrief.title: By end of shift, you can
points: Read a model's mood from its first sentence
Pick the right role for the right turn
Spec a JSON schema Echo will sign off on
Clear a capstone with hearts to spare

aside.nextStepBlurb: Pick up where you left off. The next shift is queued and Echo's standing by.

doItems: Read a short scene. Vega vs. Echo on the bridge
Drag two examples into a prompt skeleton
Run the prompt; let Echo grade 3 cases

dashboard.heading: Your progress
dashboard.signInToTrack: Sign in to save your progress between sessions.

banner.title: Welcome back, Officer. <mentor>Vega</mentor> has prepped your shift.
banner.statusBody: Status: <online>● ONLINE</online> · 3 quests pending · capstone in 2 steps

practice.title: PRACTICE
practice.empty: Nothing pending. Clear a lesson or today's mission to feed the queue.

anon.heading: Bridge closed
anon.body: Sign in to access your bridge. your progress, your next shift, and the crew you'll work with all live there.

greet.messageInProgress: You have <accent>a pending practice</accent> from yesterday. and I want it clean.
greet.messageNextUp: <accent>A new shift</accent> is queued on your path.
greet.messageDone: You've cleared everything available. <accent>Waiting on the next track</accent>.

daily.title: Daily quest
daily.body: Fix three fuzzy prompts before Echo flags them. Forge gives Forge-tokens.

capstone.title: "Talk your way past the bridge"
capstone.body: Atlas is waiting. Pass Few-shot and Schemas first.
capstone.bodyDynamic: From {track}. clear the path and run the boss.
capstone.unlocksDynamic: {steps, plural, =1 {Unlocks in 1 step} other {Unlocks in # steps}}
capstone.allClearedBody: Every track with a capstone is signed off. Wait for the next track to drop or replay anything you want to sharpen.

messages.vega: Ready when you are. No rush.
messages.forge: I'm wiring up tools. Don't break the bench.
messages.orbit: Flow mapped out. Three steps, one branch, one loop.
messages.hex: Found a crack. Sending you the repro.

activity.empty: No activity yet. Start a shift and it'll show up here.

profile.greeting: Hey {name}. bridge crew here. Keep that streak alive!
profile.bio: "Trying to convince a fleet of robots I belong here. So far: streak holding, no spills."

badges.title: Badges
badges.titleAccent: {earned, plural, =0 {no badges} one {# badge} other {# badges}}
badges.empty: Close every step of a course to earn your first badge.

activity.title: Activity
activity.empty: No activity yet. Every closed shift will appear here.
activity.logSubtitle: {n, plural, one {# shift logged} other {# shifts logged}}

context.meta: {n, plural, =0 {no officers} one {# officer} other {# officers}}

status.leadingSub: One more step keeps the gap.
status.aheadHeadline: You're <num>{xp} XP</num> from the next spot.
status.aheadSub: {name} leads you by {xp} XP. one step closes it.

rankings.title: All officers · 7 days
rankings.empty: No officers yet. Be the first to close a step.

aside.mascotLine: Three steps a day keeps the demote zone away.

empty.moversBody: When officers climb or slip, you'll see them here.

practice.eyebrow: Practice queue · {n, plural, =0 {nothing pending} one {# step} other {# steps}}
practice.titleAccent: Go back to the steps that pushed back.
practice.subheading: Every red step lands here for 30 days. Echo also brings back steps you've cleared so they don't rust on you.

group.count: {n, plural, =0 {nothing pending} one {# step} other {# steps}}

row.attempts: {n, plural, one {# failed attempt} other {# failed attempts}}

review.count: {n, plural, =0 {nothing to review} one {# step to review} other {# steps to review}}
review.body: Echo keeps a tab on what you've cleared and brings it back before it rusts. Every confirmed review stretches the next visit.

row.interval: {days, plural, one {# day} other {# day}} cycle
row.streak: {n, plural, =0 {No streak} one {# review in a row} other {# reviews in a row}}

empty.title: Queue is clean.
empty.body: Nothing pending. As soon as a step pushes back, it lands here for review. To bank XP, keep moving forward on the map.

noneAvailable.title: No mission available.
noneAvailable.body: No daily missions are published in this language yet. Keep moving forward on the map to hold your streak.

successCard.body: You cleared all {count} scenes. Come back tomorrow for the next one.

errors.invalid_input: Your answer didn't reach the server cleanly.
errors.wrong_quest: That's not the mission you were assigned today.
errors.unsupported_kind: This mission isn't compatible with daily mode.
errors.scene_count_mismatch: The server got a different number of answers than expected. Try again.
errors.no_hearts: You're out of hearts. Wait for regen or stop by the workbench.

crew.subheading: Each one teaches something different and has their own character. Your bond grows with every step you clear alongside them.

lessonsDialog.eyebrow: {count, plural, =0 {no lessons} one {# lesson} other {# lessons}}
lessonsDialog.title: {name} in class
lessonsDialog.description: Every lesson where {name} participates as guide, evaluator, or part of the scene.
lessonsDialog.empty: {name} does not appear in a published lesson yet.

bond.stepsToNext: {n, plural, one {# step to} other {# steps to}} {tier}

vega.descriptionShort: Welcomes you aboard. Teaches you how to give an instruction that can't be misread.
vega.tagline: Easy. One question at a time.
vega.bio: Vega meets you on the deck. Zero judgment when you mess up. she just asks you to try again with one more detail. Most officers learn with her first because her patience threshold is absurd.
quirks: Likes it when you include examples before asking.
If your prompt is ambiguous, she returns the simplest possible question.
Always calls you "officer". Never your name.

echo.descriptionShort: Verifies data. Speaks in checklists. Loves clean formats.
echo.bio: Echo isn't cruel. they're strict. If your prompt passes, they tell you in four words. If it fails, they tell you exactly why with an example from the run. They learned to be brief from a human who said "don't explain, just tell me if it passed".
quirks: No adjectives. "Good" means passed, "bad" means failed.
Perfect memory of every prompt you've sent before.
Sends responses just as fast at night.

atlas.descriptionShort: The one who signs off on your first report. Demands tight structure and few words.
atlas.tagline: This goes on the record. Three runs, no misses.
atlas.bio: Atlas doesn't appear until the capstone. When he does, he gives you a brief and goes quiet. If you pass, he doesn't celebrate. he nods and logs it. If you fail, he explains it once. Then you have to go back to Vega or Echo.
quirks: Speaks in single-line messages. Never two.
If you fail, he doesn't insist. sends you back to Vega or Echo.
When he logs something, he says it: "on the record". His tic.

forge.descriptionShort: Builds the tools the crew uses. Loves explaining how things work under the hood.
forge.tagline: Let's break something. Then we fix it.
forge.bio: Forge teaches tools and MCPs. She learned to code by talking to a system that only answered when tool descriptions were good. Zero patience for vague descriptions, infinite patience for broken code.
quirks: She'll hand you the first broken tool on purpose.
Likes short but clear variable names.
If your prompt doesn't produce the expected tool call, she shows you the trace.

orbit.descriptionShort: Breaks problems into steps. Decides what runs, in what order, and when to stop.
orbit.tagline: One step at a time. But every step.
orbit.voice: Methodical · diagrams in her head · patient with loops
orbit.bio: Orbit doesn't solve the task. she designs how it gets solved. When you bring her a big problem the first thing she does is ask what subtasks live inside it. Then she decides which run in sequence, which branch, and when the loop has to cut. She's bored by single-prompt problems.
quirks: Before coding, she sketches the flow on the whiteboard.
Asks 'what happens if this fails?' at every step. Always.
If your agent enters an infinite loop, she tells you without laughing.

hex.descriptionShort: Breaks systems on purpose so they don't break in production. Thinks like an attacker.
hex.tagline: If I break it in 10 minutes, someone else breaks it in less.
hex.teaches: Prompt injection · Data leaks · Tool red-teaming · Confident hallucinations
hex.bio: Hex isn't paranoid. they're professional. Their job is to assume the input is hostile and find out what happens when it is. They work alongside Atlas: he signs off on what you ship, they're the only reason he's willing to sign. If your prompt leaves a crack, Hex finds it before your user does.
quirks: Asks for one destructive input before reviewing your prompt.
Never says 'you passed'. Says 'didn't find anything this time'.
Keeps a list of attacks seen in production. Reviews it with you.

tutor.title: AI Tutor
tutor.opener: I'm here. Step and exercise are open on my console. ask what you need.

anon.title: Sign in to chat
anon.body: The AI tutor is reserved for officers with an account. Create one in 30 seconds to start the conversation.

errors.unauthorized: Sign in to chat with the tutor.
errors.rate_limited: You've hit today's quota. Come back tomorrow.
errors.not_found: We couldn't load this step's context.
errors.invalid_input: The message you sent isn't valid.
errors.model_error: The tutor couldn't respond. Try again.
errors.network: Something went wrong sending your message. Try again.

errors.unauthorized: Sign in to save your attempts.
errors.rateLimited: Too many attempts today. Try again tomorrow.
errors.invalidInput: The submitted answer is not valid.
errors.noHearts: You're out of hearts. Wait for the next regen or refill in the shop.
errors.modelError: The model didn't respond. Try again.
errors.network: Something went wrong submitting your answer. Try again.

zeroHearts.label: OUT OF HEARTS
zeroHearts.body: You're out of hearts. Check is blocked until tomorrow's refill. In the meantime, you can practice freely in the <workbench>workbench</workbench> without spending anything.

anon.label: GUEST MODE
anon.body: You're viewing this lesson as a guest. To save your progress, earn XP, and keep your streak, sign in when you're ready to check.

assemble.failed: Echo found blocks out of place.

anatomy.label: DRAG TO LABEL
anatomy.question: Tag each block of the prompt. Drag the labels above onto the block they describe.
anatomy.passed: Labels are correct. Echo signs off.

ab.label: MCQ · NO COST
ab.rubricHint: Look for: closed contract, explicit fallback, scaffold at the end.

tagFill.label: FILL THE SLOT · 1 HOLE
tagFill.question: Edit the content between the tags of the prompt.

toolSchemaAuthor.label: DEFINE THE SCHEMA
toolSchemaAuthor.instruction: Write the JSON Schema for the {tool} tool.
toolSchemaAuthor.passed: Schema approved. Echo registers it as a valid tool.
toolSchemaAuthor.failed: Schema doesn't pass all criteria yet. adjust and check again.

toolHandlerImplement.label: IMPLEMENT THE HANDLER
toolHandlerImplement.instruction: Implement the handler for the {tool} tool.
toolHandlerImplement.passed: Handler approved. Expected behavior looks correct.
toolHandlerImplement.failed: Handler doesn't pass all criteria yet. reread the scenarios and adjust.

prompt.label: Your transmission
prompt.hint: use {token} where the input should go

crew.label: Echo diagnosis
crew.promptTask: The test is not checking whether the prompt sounds nice. It checks whether it produces consistent outputs across several cases. Change one thing per attempt and compare the telemetry.
crew.promptTaskPassed: Clean run. Echo logged every case.

lessonComplete.title: Logged
lessonComplete.body: You're on probation with the crew. The next phase of onboarding is waiting.

outputGallery.title: Failure gallery
outputGallery.reveal: Before the reveal: what went wrong with this output?

promptAnnotate.title: Tap each part to see what it does
promptAnnotate.revealLabel: Pick a part to see what it does.

toolCallTrace.title: The model decides turn by turn

chainFlow.title: Watch data flow through the chain
chainFlow.selectHint: Tap a node or play the chain to inspect it.

prompt.label: Prompt
prompt.placeholder: Write a prompt and run the transmission

output.label: Response
output.empty: Run a transmission to see the output.

crew.label: Echo note
crew.defaultNote: Run several versions of the same prompt and compare what changed in the output. The real practice is noticing which part of your instruction caused each response.

history.label: Transmission history
history.empty: No runs yet. Run the prompt to save a local version.

conversation.emptyTranscript: The conversation starts when you send your first message.
conversation.inputPlaceholder: Type your message and send with ⌘/Ctrl+Enter
conversation.noTurnsLeft: No turns left. Check the conversation.

errors.rate_limited: Too many attempts today. Try again tomorrow.
errors.model_error: The model couldn't respond. Try again.
errors.network: Something went wrong sending the message. Try again.

confirm.title: Asking for a hint costs 1 heart
confirm.body: Hints help you understand the exercise, but they cost a heart. Hearts come back over time or from the Shop. If you meant to move on, use the Next button in the bottom right instead.

debrief.title: Mission cleared.
debrief.subtitle: Good shift, officer. {officer} signed off. you rose from Ensign to Lieutenant Jr.

capstone.title: Capstone in the bag.
capstone.subtitle: Bridge clean. {officer} stamped your file. you cleared the boss without burning the ship.

toolDesc.instruction: Edit the description of {tool} so the agent knows when to call it.
toolDesc.failed: Key phrases are missing from the description.

mcpDebug.question: Look at the spec. What's the most likely reason the agent never picks this tool?
mcpDebug.failed: Echo points to a different root cause.

checks.json:invalid: The output is not valid JSON.
checks.json:not-object: The JSON is not an object.
checks.regex:no-match: The output format doesn't match what was expected.
checks.contains:not-found: The output doesn't contain the expected text.
checks.length:too-short: The output is shorter than expected ({len} characters).
checks.length:too-long: The output is longer than expected ({len} characters).
checks.xml-malformed: The XML is not well-formed (an unclosed tag).
checks.missing-input-placeholder: Your prompt doesn't use the {'{{input}}'} placeholder. Insert it where each test case's input should go; otherwise every case gets the same prompt.
checks.task-model:error: The model failed to produce an output. Try again.
checks.judge:rate-limited: The automated grader is overloaded. Try again in a minute.
checks.judge:invalid-shape: The automated grader returned an unexpected response.
checks.judge:unknown-error: The automated grader had an unknown problem.
checks.skipped:deterministic-failed: Pending. fix the prior criteria first.

blog.title: How to coordinate with AI
blog.subtitle: Guides, tutorials, and comparisons on prompting, skills, MCP, agents, and the toolkit for shipping with applied AI.
blog.empty: No posts yet in this category. Check back soon.

meta.listTitle: Naveo Logbook · Prompting, skills, MCP and agents
meta.listDescription: Learn how to build with applied AI: prompting, Claude Skills, MCP servers, agents, and workflows. Tutorials and comparisons in English.

cta.title: Stop reading. Start coordinating with the crew.
cta.body: What you just read clicks once you practice it. Step onto the bridge and run Naveo's first track.

meta.title: Terms and conditions · Naveo
meta.description: Rules for using Naveo: account, user content, AI features, and limitations.

terms.title: Terms and conditions
terms.intro: These terms govern the use of Naveo, a gamified learning platform for applied AI and basic programming available at naveo.space. By creating an account or using the site you accept these conditions. If you do not agree, do not use Naveo.

sections.title: 1. What Naveo is
sections.body: Naveo offers interactive lessons, hands-on exercises, and daily quests to learn prompting, MCPs, agents, and workflows with language models. Content is delivered with game mechanics (XP, streak, hearts, gems) that exist only inside the platform and carry no monetary value outside it.

sections.title: 2. Who can use the service
sections.body: You must be at least 13 years old to create an account. If you are between 13 and the age of majority in your country, you need permission from a parent or legal guardian. In the European Union the minimum age is 16, unless local law sets a different threshold. By using Naveo you confirm you meet this requirement.

sections.title: 3. Your account
sections.body: Access is managed through Clerk. You are responsible for keeping your credentials safe and for all activity that happens under your account. If you spot unauthorized access, email us at hola@naveo.space and we will lock the session. We can suspend accounts that break these terms or put other users at risk.

sections.title: 4. Acceptable use
sections.body: You may not use Naveo to submit illegal, harmful, defamatory, or rights-infringing content. Do not try to break the site's security, scrape content at scale, automate lesson completion, or use the API or models for purposes outside of learning (for example, generating spam or malware). Abuse of AI credits may lead to account suspension.

sections.title: 5. Content you generate
sections.body: When you write prompts, exercise answers, or comments, that content remains yours. You grant us a worldwide, royalty-free, non-exclusive license to store it, process it through language model providers, and show it back to you as part of your progress. We may analyze it in aggregate and anonymized form to improve the platform, evaluation criteria, and lesson content.

sections.title: 6. AI features
sections.body: Naveo routes requests to AI models through OpenRouter and providers such as Anthropic, OpenAI, and others. Responses are generated automatically, may contain errors, inaccuracies, or biases, and do not constitute professional advice. Do not use the outputs for legal, medical, financial, or safety decisions without human verification. Model availability may change.

sections.title: 7. XP, streak, hearts, and gems
sections.body: Game metrics are pedagogical signals, not currency. They have no monetary value and cannot be sold, transferred, or redeemed for real money. We can tune the system rules (how much XP a lesson awards, shop prices, and so on) to balance the experience.

sections.title: 8. Intellectual property
sections.body: Naveo's code, design, brand, illustrations, lessons, evaluation criteria, and other material belong to Naveo or its licensors. You may access and use the content for personal learning. You may not copy, redistribute, sell, or create derivative works for commercial purposes without written permission.

sections.title: 9. Changes to the service
sections.body: Naveo is under active development. We may add, change, or remove features, lessons, tracks, and quests at any time. If we make a significant change that affects your account (for example, removing content you paid for), we will notify you by email or from your dashboard.

sections.title: 10. Termination
sections.body: You can close your account at any time from your profile or by writing to hola@naveo.space. We can suspend or terminate your access if you break these terms. When you close your account, your progress is deleted following the timelines described in the privacy policy.

sections.title: 11. Warranties and limitation of liability
sections.body: Naveo is provided "as is", without express or implied warranties. We do not guarantee that the service will be available without interruption, free of bugs, or that AI outputs will be correct. To the extent permitted by law, Naveo is not liable for indirect damages, lost profits, lost data, or any harm derived from the use or inability to use the service.

sections.title: 12. Governing law
sections.body: These terms are governed by the laws applicable where Naveo operates. Any dispute will be resolved before the competent courts of that jurisdiction, unless consumer protection law grants you additional rights.

sections.title: 13. Contact
sections.body: If you have questions about these terms, write to hola@naveo.space. If we update the document, we will change the date at the top of the page and, when the change is material, notify you on the site.

meta.title: Privacy policy · Naveo
meta.description: What data Naveo collects, what we use it for, who we share it with, and your rights.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.intro: This policy explains what personal data we collect when you use Naveo at naveo.space, how we handle it, who we share it with, and what rights you have. It is part of the terms and conditions of the service.

sections.title: 1. Data controller
sections.body: The data controller is Naveo. For any privacy question, write to hola@naveo.space.

sections.title: 2. Data we collect
sections.body: Account data: email, name, photo, and sign-in provider managed by Clerk. Usage data: lesson progress, prompts you write, answers you submit, completed exercises, XP, streak, hearts, gems, and shop purchases. Technical data: IP address, device type, browser, language, and date and time of requests. Cookies: strictly necessary ones to sign you in and remember your language.

sections.title: 3. Why we use your data
sections.body: To deliver the service: authenticate you, save your progress, show you the next lesson, run your prompts, and return the model output. To improve content: review in aggregate which lessons work best, which evaluation criteria fail, which errors appear. To talk with you: account notices, important changes, replies to your messages. For security: detect abuse, prevent fraud, and protect the platform.

sections.title: 4. Legal basis
sections.body: We process your data to perform the contract you accept when using Naveo (the terms and conditions), to comply with legal obligations, and in some cases under our legitimate interest in maintaining and improving the service. When the law requires it, we ask for explicit consent (for example, optional marketing emails).

sections.title: 5. Who we share data with
sections.body: Only with the providers strictly needed for the service to work: Clerk (authentication and user management), OpenRouter together with Anthropic, OpenAI, and other language model providers (they process the prompts you send to generate the response), the PostgreSQL database provider, and the hosting infrastructure. Each one processes your data under its own data protection agreements. We do not sell your personal information.

sections.title: 6. International transfers
sections.body: Some providers are located outside your country (mainly in the United States and the European Union). In those cases we rely on standard contractual clauses and other safeguards approved by data protection authorities.

sections.title: 7. Retention
sections.body: We keep your data while your account is active. If you close the account, we erase identifying data within a maximum of 90 days, keeping only the records the law forces us to retain (for example, billing) and fully anonymized usage metrics.

sections.title: 8. Your rights
sections.body: You can access, rectify, delete, object to, or restrict the processing of your data, and request portability. Write to hola@naveo.space to exercise any of these rights. You can also file a complaint with the data protection authority in your country if you believe we did not handle your request properly.

sections.title: 9. Cookies
sections.body: We use only functional cookies: one to keep your session signed in (Clerk) and another to remember your language. We do not use advertising or third-party tracking cookies for marketing purposes.

sections.title: 10. Minors
sections.body: Naveo is not aimed at children under 13. If we discover that an account was created without the proper consent of a parent or guardian, we will delete it along with the associated data.

sections.title: 11. Security
sections.body: We apply reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect your data: encryption in transit, hashed passwords managed by the authentication provider, role-restricted access, and audit logs. No system is 100% bulletproof, so we recommend using unique passwords and enabling two-step verification with your sign-in provider.

sections.title: 12. Changes to this policy
sections.body: If we update this policy, we will change the date at the top and, if the change is significant, notify you by email or from the dashboard before it takes effect.

sections.title: 13. Contact
sections.body: For any question about privacy or to exercise your rights, write to hola@naveo.space.
